lede: A look at April 9 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including TommyInnit and Jazmine Sullivan, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Tatmadaw
events.desc: Burmese military and security forces commit the Bago massacre, during which at least 82 civilians are killed.

events.title: Irish Republican Army
events.desc: An IRA bombing in County Down, Northern Ireland, kills three members of the UDR.

events.title: Boeing 737
events.desc: The first Boeing 737 (a 100 series) makes its maiden flight.

events.title: Tornado outbreak of April 9–11, 1947
events.desc: The Glazier–Higgins–Woodward tornadoes kill 181 and injure 970 in Texas, Oklahoma, and Kansas.

events.title: Journey of Reconciliation
events.desc: The Journey of Reconciliation, the first interracial Freedom Ride begins through the upper South in violation of Jim Crow laws. The riders wanted enforcement of the United States Supreme Court's 1946 Irene Morgan decision that banned racial segregation in interstate travel.

events.title: United Nations Security Council Resolution 22
events.desc: United Nations Security Council Resolution 22 relating to Corfu Channel incident is adopted.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Operation Weserübung: Germany invades Denmark and Norway.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: The Battle of Arras: The battle begins with Canadian Corps executing a massive assault on Vimy Ridge.

events.title: Phonautograph
events.desc: On his phonautograph machine, Édouard-Léon Scott de Martinville makes the first known recording of an audible human voice.

events.title: Old Swiss Confederacy
events.desc: Despite being outnumbered 16:1, forces of the Old Swiss Confederacy are victorious over the Archduchy of Austria in the Battle of Näfels.

births.name: Bernard Jenkin
births.line: English businessman and politician, Shadow Secretary of State for Defence

births.name: Abraham Ribicoff
births.line: American lawyer and politician, 4th United States Secretary of Health and Human Services (died 1998)

births.name: Charles Winckler
births.line: Danish tug of war competitor, discus thrower, and shot putter (died 1932)

deaths.text: Ray Shero, American ice hockey player and executive
Billy Hitchcock, American baseball player, coach, manager
Ibrahim Baré Maïnassara, Nigerien general and politician, President of Niger
Muhammad Baqir al-Sadr, Iraqi cleric and philosopher

observances.text: Christian feast day: Blessed Thomas of Tolentino
